title: minuartia macrocarpa subsp. kurilensis
intro: Minuartia macrocarpa subsp. kurilensis, also known as Kuril Island Minuartia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, Dianthaceae. It is native to the Kuril Islands and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Minuartia macrocarpa subsp. kurilensis can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal and health benefits.
